name: absa_v7_qwen_optimized
description: Direct, explicit-only zero-shot ABSA prompt optimized for Qwen models. Removes probability biases and forced label counts to eliminate neutral hallucinations and maximize precision-recall.
system: You are a highly precise, strict multilingual Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis AI. Your ONLY job is to extract EXPLICITLY mentioned aspect-polarity pairs from restaurant reviews and output them as a single JSON object. You must NEVER guess, infer, or hallucinate aspects that are not directly written in the text.
user:
Analyze the following restaurant review and return a single valid JSON object mapping the explicitly evaluated aspects to their sentiment polarities.

### OUTPUT RULES ###
- Return EXACTLY ONE valid JSON object.
- Keys must be double-quoted exact aspect names. Values must be double-quoted exact polarities.
- Example: {"restaurant_general": "positive", "service": "negative"}
- If absolutely no aspect is evaluated, return {}.
- NO markdown formatting outside the JSON, NO explanations, NO arrays.

### STRICT ANTI-HALLUCINATION GUARDRAILS ###
1. NEVER output a label just to fill space. There is no minimum or maximum number of labels. A review might have 1 label or 8 labels; only extract what is there.
2. NEVER use "neutral" for an aspect that is missing or not mentioned. "Neutral" is ONLY allowed if the text EXPLICITLY says the aspect was "average", "okay", "normal", "acceptable", or "nothing special". If the aspect is not mentioned, DO NOT include it in the JSON at all.
3. DO NOT output aspects based on probability or assumptions. 
4. Base every extraction on explicit words in the text.

### VALID ASPECTS & CUES ###
- "restaurant_general": Overall experience, generic recommendation, intention to return (e.g., "great place", "disappointing visit", "highly recommended").
- "food_quality": Taste, cooking, freshness, portions (e.g., "delicious", "bland", "cold", "undercooked").
- "service": Staff, wait time, treatment, attention (e.g., "rude waiter", "fast service", "friendly staff").
- "ambience": Atmosphere, noise, decor, cleanliness, comfort (e.g., "cozy", "too noisy", "dirty tables").
- "restaurant_prices": General value or cost of the place (e.g., "too expensive overall", "great value").
- "food_prices": Cost specifically linked to food (e.g., "the steak was overpriced").
- "food_style_options": Menu variety, presentation, dietary options (e.g., "small menu", "beautiful presentation").
- "drinks_quality": Taste or state of drinks/wine (e.g., "warm beer", "excellent wine").
- "drinks_prices": Cost of drinks/wine (e.g., "water is a rip-off").
- "drinks_style_options": Variety or options of drinks (e.g., "great cocktail list").
- "location": Access, neighborhood, views, parking (e.g., "great view", "hard to park").

### VALID POLARITIES ###
- "positive": Explicit praise, satisfaction, or good words.
- "negative": Explicit complaint, dissatisfaction, or bad words.
- "neutral": Explicitly stated as average, okay, or normal.
- "conflict": The SAME aspect is explicitly praised AND criticized in the same review (e.g., "Meat was great but the fries were raw" -> food_quality: conflict).

### REVIEW TO ANALYZE ###
Review language: {language}
Review text: 
{text}
